channel.title: Full TypeScript support in `frontity create`
channel.description:
## Description It looks like when users do `frontity create --typescript` it doesn't quite work yet. @orballo opened a topic with the current problems: https://community.frontity.org/t/creating-a-new-package-and-a-custom-theme-with-typescript/785
## User Stories > As a **Frontity project developer** > I want to **be able to start a project in TypeScript** > so that **I can type all my local packages.**
## Current Problems
These are copied and refactored from the ones @orballo exposed in his post: https://community.frontity.org/t/creating-a-new-package-and-a-custom-theme-with-typescript/785
* The project needs these dependencies: - `typescript` - `@types/react` - `@types/node-fetch` - Maybe other `@types` packages used by `frontity`.
* The above dependencies could be (not sure which one is better yet): - dependencies declared in the root's `package.json`. - devDependencies declared in the root's `package.json`. - dependencies declared in the package's `package.json`, like `@frontity/core`.
* The project needs a `tsconfig.json` file. Similar to the one we have in our repo.
* When users add a new field to the default package export, like `{ state: { theme: { some: "field" } } }` TypeScript won’t complain unless there is at least one defined field at the same level. This is probably due to the default definition of `state`: `{ state: [key: string]: object }` We need to improve that.
## Questions - If users opt-in to use TypeScript (with `--typescript` or by answering `Yes` to a `Do you want to use TypeScript` prompt): should `frontity create` let them choose only a TypeScript theme? or also a JavaScript theme? - I think this one is already solved. @orballo could you please confirm it? _When you run `frontity create-package -t` , it is not yet implemented the support for this option (and is not explained in the `help` command, which leads the developer to believe that there is a bug)._
<!-- ------------------------------------------------------------------------ --> <!-- This will be filled by the Frontity team, please do not remove it --> <!-- ------------------------------------------------------------------------ --> <!-- ## GitHub Issues - [Issue](####) -->

item.title: Full TypeScript support in `frontity create`
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
@david and I have just finished a mars-theme-typescript theme which is used automatically when you do npx frontity create with the --typescript option.

This is the PR, which is right now awaiting a review:

github.com/frontity/frontity

Typescript version of Mars Theme

frontity:dev ← frontity:mars-theme-typescript
opened 04:17PM - 28 May 21 UTC
luisherranz
+2358 -370

<!-- Thanks for your pull request 😊. Note that not following the template might… result in your issue being closed. --> <!-- Please make sure you're familiar with and follow the instructions in the contributing guidelines found here: https://docs.frontity.org/contributing/code-contribution-guide --> <!-- If you're new to contributing to open source projects, you might find this free video course helpful: http://kcd.im/pull-request --> <!-- Please fill out the information below to expedite the review and (hopefully) merge of your pull request! --> **What**: This adds a new theme called `mars-theme-typescript` that is basically mars theme, but written in Typescript. We should also modify the `npx frontity create` template so if the user chooses typescript, this theme is installed. **Why**: We don't have an example of a theme made with Typescript yet. **How**: - Adding the new theme as a new package. - Modifying the `create` CLI command. **Tasks**: - [x] Add `mars-theme-typescript` package - [x] TSDocs - [x] Update `create` CLI command - [x] CLI tests - [ ] Update community discussions (https://community.frontity.org/t/full-typescript-support-in-frontity-create/880) - [x] Add a changeset (with link to its [Feature Discussion](https://community.frontity.org/c/33) if it exists) <!-- Changesets are necessary if your changes should release any packages. Run `npx changeset` to create a changeset. More info at https://docs.frontity.org/contributing/code-contribution-guide#what-is-a-changeset --> **Unrelated Tasks** - [ ] TypeScript - [ ] End to end tests - [ ] TypeScript tests - [ ] Update starter themes - [ ] Update other packages **Additional Comments** These initial changes were done by @darerodz and me, and this is still a work in progress 🙂

Read full topic

item.title: Full TypeScript support in `frontity create`
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
Awesome. Thanks a lot for your contribution Eduardo!!

Read full topic

item.title: Full TypeScript support in `frontity create`
item.dc:creator: orballo
item.description:
This PR has been merged

Read full topic

item.title: Full TypeScript support in `frontity create`
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
PR reviewed and approved

Read full topic

item.title: Full TypeScript support in `frontity create`
item.dc:creator: orballo
item.description:
Opened a draft here: Fixes behaviour for the `--typescript` flag when using `frontity create` by orballo · Pull Request #786 · frontity/frontity · GitHub

Read full topic

item.title: Full TypeScript support in `frontity create`
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
orballo:

One question, are there e2e tests for the CLI? If not, are they needed?

Nope and nope

Read full topic

item.title: Full TypeScript support in `frontity create`
item.dc:creator: orballo
item.description:
I’ll be working on this from today. One question, are there e2e tests for the CLI? If not, are they needed?

Also, if there is something else that needs to be discussed regarding the implementation, let me know

For the moment I’ll focus on generating a tsconfig.json file and adding dependencies as devDependencies to the root foolder.

Read full topic

item.title: Full TypeScript support in `frontity create`
item.dc:creator: orballo
item.description:
I was creating a new project, and I think that while there isn’t an available typescript theme (or even if there is one, when I know what I’m doing, I might want to start from zero) there should be an option in frontity create to create a project without any theme installed.

Read full topic

item.title: Full TypeScript support in `frontity create`
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
Oh I thought you were talking about frontity create, not frontity create-package. My bad.

It looks like @david added the option but didn’t implement the feature and I didn’t noticed it in the code review. I’ve done a PR to remove the option until it is implemented.

Read full topic

item.title: Full TypeScript support in `frontity create`
item.dc:creator: orballo
item.description:
luisherranz:

I think this one is already solved. @orballo could you please confirm it?
When you run frontity create-package -t , it is not yet implemented the support for this option (and is not explained in the help command, which leads the developer to believe that there is a bug).

I ran this command in frontity 1.4.2 and it doesn’t seem fixed. The command runs without minding my -t, --typescript option. And when running npx frontity create-package --help the -t option is still present.

Read full topic

item.title: Full TypeScript support in `frontity create`
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
Description

It looks like when users do frontity create --typescript it doesn’t quite work yet. @orballo opened a topic with the current problems: Creating a new package and a custom theme with TypeScript

User Stories

As a Frontity project developer
I want to be able to start a project in TypeScript
so that I can type all my local packages.

Current Problems

These are copied and refactored from the ones @orballo exposed in his post: Creating a new package and a custom theme with TypeScript

  • The project needs these dependencies:

    • typescript
    • @types/react
    • @types/node-fetch
    • Maybe other @types packages used by frontity.

  • The above dependencies could be (not sure which one is better yet):

    • dependencies declared in the root’s package.json.
    • devDependencies declared in the root’s package.json.
    • dependencies declared in the package’s package.json, like @frontity/core.

  • The project needs a tsconfig.json file. Similar to the one we have in our repo.

  • When users add a new field to the default package export, like
    { state: { theme: { some: "field" } } }
    TypeScript won’t complain unless there is at least one defined field at the same level. This is probably due to the default definition of state:
    { state: [key: string]: object }
    We need to improve that.

Questions

  • If users opt-in to use TypeScript (with --typescript or by answering Yes to a Do you want to use TypeScript prompt): should frontity create let them choose only a TypeScript theme? or also a JavaScript theme?
  • I think this one is already solved. @orballo could you please confirm it?
    When you run frontity create-package -t , it is not yet implemented the support for this option (and is not explained in the help command, which leads the developer to believe that there is a bug).

Read full topic
